Temporary Special Rules for Health and Dependent Care Flexible Spending Arrangements, , , provided that:Pub. L. 116–260, div. EE, title II, § 214134 Stat. 3068Dec. 27, 2020“(a)2020.—Carryover FromPlan YearFor plan years ending in 2020, a plan that includes a health flexible spending arrangement or dependent care flexible spending arrangement shall not fail to be treated as a cafeteria plan under the Internal Revenue Code of 1986 merely because such plan or arrangement permits participants to carry over (under rules similar to the rules applicable to health flexible spending arrangements) any unused benefits or contributions remaining in any such flexible spending arrangement from such plan year to the plan year ending in 2021.“(b)2021.—Carryover FromPlan YearFor plan years ending in 2021, a plan that includes a health flexible spending arrangement or dependent care flexible spending arrangement shall not fail to be treated as a cafeteria plan under the Internal Revenue Code of 1986 merely because such plan or arrangement permits participants to carry over (under rules similar to the rules applicable to health flexible spending arrangements) any unused benefits or contributions remaining in any such flexible spending arrangement from such plan year to the plan year ending in 2022.“(c)Extension of Grace Periods, etc.—“(1).—In generalA plan that includes a health flexible spending arrangement or dependent care flexible spending arrangement shall not fail to be treated as a cafeteria plan under the Internal Revenue Code of 1986 merely because such plan or arrangement extends the grace period for a plan year ending in 2020 or 2021 to 12 months after the end of such plan year, with respect to unused benefits or contributions remaining in a health flexible spending arrangement or a dependent care flexible spending arrangement.“(2).—Post-termination reimbursements from health FSAsA plan that includes a health flexible spending arrangement shall not fail to be treated as a cafeteria plan under the Internal Revenue Code of 1986 merely because such plan or arrangement allows (under rules similar to the rules applicable to dependent care flexible spending arrangements) an employee who ceases participation in the plan during calendar year 2020 or 2021 to continue to receive reimbursements from unused benefits or contributions through the end of the plan year in which such participation ceased (including any grace period, taking into account any modification of a grace period permitted under paragraph (1)).“(d)Special Carry Forward Rule for Dependent Care Flexible Spending Arrangements Where Dependent Aged Out During Pandemic.—“(1).—In generalIn the case of any eligible employee, section 21(b)(1)(A) of the Internal Revenue Code of 1986 shall be applied by substituting ‘age 14’ for ‘age 13’ for purposes of determining the dependent care assistance which may be paid or reimbursed with respect to such employee under the dependent care flexible spending arrangement referred to in paragraph (3)(A) with respect to such employee during—“(A)the plan year described in paragraph (3)(A), and“(B)in the case of an employee described in paragraph (3)(B)(ii), the subsequent plan year.“(2).—Application to subsequent plan year limited to unused balance from preceding plan yearParagraph (1)(B) shall only apply to so much of the amounts paid for dependent care assistance with respect to the dependents referred to in paragraph (3)(B) as does not exceed the unused balance described in paragraph (3)(B)(ii).“(3).—Eligible employeeFor purposes of this section, the term ‘eligible employee’ means any employee who—“(A)is enrolled in a dependent care flexible spending arrangement for the last plan year with respect to which the end of the regular enrollment period for such plan year was on or before , andJanuary 31, 2020“(B)has one or more dependents (as defined in section 152(a)(1) of the Internal Revenue Code of 1986) who attain the age of 13—“(i)during such plan year, or“(ii)in the case of an employee who (after the application of this section) has an unused balance in the employee’s account under such arrangement for such plan year (determined as of the close of the last day on which, under the terms of the plan, claims for reimbursement may be made with respect to such plan year), the subsequent plan year.“(e).—Change in Election AmountFor plan years ending in 2021, a plan that includes a health flexible spending arrangement or dependent care flexible spending arrangement shall not fail to be treated as a cafeteria plan under the Internal Revenue Code of 1986 merely because such plan or arrangement allows an employee to make an election to modify prospectively the amount (but not in excess of any applicable dollar limitation) of such employee’s contributions to any such flexible spending arrangement (without regard to any change in status).“(f).—DefinitionsAny term used in this section which is also used in section 106, 125, or 129 of the Internal Revenue Code of 1986, or the regulations or guidance thereunder, shall have the same meaning as when used in such section, regulations, or guidance.“(g).—Plan AmendmentsA plan that includes a health flexible spending arrangement or dependent care flexible spending arrangement shall not fail to be treated as a cafeteria plan under the Internal Revenue Code of 1986 merely because such plan or arrangement is amended pursuant to a provision under this section and such amendment is retroactive, if—“(1)such amendment is adopted not later than the last day of the first calendar year beginning after the end of the plan year in which the amendment is effective, and“(2)the plan or arrangement is operated consistent with the terms of such amendment during the period beginning on the effective date of the amendment and ending on the date the amendment is adopted.”

made with respect to such plan year), the subsequent plan year.“(e).—Change in Election AmountFor plan years ending in 2021, a plan that includes a health flexible spending arrangement or dependent care flexible spending arrangement shall not fail to be treated as a cafeteria plan under the Internal Revenue Code of 1986 merely because such plan or arrangement allows an employee to make an election to modify prospectively the amount (but not in excess of any applicable dollar limitation) of such employee’s contributions to any such flexible spending arrangement (without regard to any children.heading: General rule
children.label: a General rule
children.text: Except as provided in subsection (b), no amount shall be included in the gross income of a participant in a cafeteria plan solely because, under the plan, the participant may choose among the benefits of the plan.

children.heading: Exception for highly compensated participants and key employees
children.label: b Exception for highly compensated participants and key employees
A
children.text: highly compensated individuals as to eligibility to participate, or

children.label: B
children.text: highly compensated participants as to contributions and benefits.

children.heading: Key employees
children.label: 2 Key employees
children.text: In the case of a key employee (within the meaning of section 416(i)(1)), subsection (a) shall not apply to any benefit attributable to a plan for which the qualified benefits provided to key employees exceed 25 percent of the aggregate of such benefits provided for all employees under the plan. For purposes of the preceding sentence, qualified benefits shall be determined without regard to the second sentence of subsection (f).

children.heading: Year of inclusion
children.label: 3 Year of inclusion
children.text: For purposes of determining the taxable year of inclusion, any benefit described in paragraph (1) or (2) shall be treated as received or accrued in the taxable year of the participant or key employee in which the plan year ends.

children.heading: Discrimination as to benefits or contributions
children.label: c Discrimination as to benefits or contributions
children.text: For purposes of subparagraph (B) of subsection (b)(1), a cafeteria plan does not discriminate where qualified benefits and total benefits (or employer contributions allocable to qualified benefits and employer contributions for total benefits) do not discriminate in favor of highly compensated participants.

children.label: A
children.text: all participants are employees, and

children.label: B
children.text: the participants may choose among 2 or more benefits consisting of cash and qualified benefits.

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: The term “cafeteria plan” does not include any plan which provides for deferred compensation.

children.heading: Exception for cash and deferred arrangements
children.label: B Exception for cash and deferred arrangements
children.text: Subparagraph (A) shall not apply to a profit-sharing or stock bonus plan or rural cooperative plan (within the meaning of section 401(k)(7)) which includes a qualified cash or deferred arrangement (as defined in section 401(k)(2)) to the extent of amounts which a covered employee may elect to have the employer pay as contributions to a trust under such plan on behalf of the employee.

children.heading: Exception for certain plans maintained by educational institutions
children.label: C Exception for certain plans maintained by educational institutions
i
children.text: all contributions for such insurance must be made before retirement, and

children.label: ii
children.text: such life insurance does not have a cash surrender value at any time.

children.heading: Exception for health savings accounts
children.label: D Exception for health savings accounts
children.text: Subparagraph (A) shall not apply to a plan to the extent of amounts which a covered employee may elect to have the employer pay as contributions to a health savings account established on behalf of the employee.

children.heading: Highly compensated participant and individual defined
children.label: e Highly compensated participant and individual defined
A
children.text: an officer,

children.label: B
children.text: a shareholder owning more than 5 percent of the voting power or value of all classes of stock of the employer,

children.label: C
children.text: highly compensated, or

children.label: D
children.text: a spouse or dependent (within the meaning of section 152, determined without regard to subsections (b)(1), (b)(2), and (d)(1)(B) thereof) of an individual described in subparagraph (A), (B), or (C).

children.heading: Highly compensated individual
children.label: 2 Highly compensated individual
children.text: The term “highly compensated individual” means an individual who is described in subparagraph (A), (B), (C), or (D) of paragraph (1).

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: The term “qualified benefit” means any benefit which, with the application of subsection (a), is not includible in the gross income of the employee by reason of an express provision of this chapter (other than section 106(b), 117, 127, or 132). Such term includes any group term life insurance which is includible in gross income only because it exceeds the dollar limitation of section 79 and such term includes any other benefit permitted under regulations.

children.heading: Long-term care insurance not qualified
children.label: 2 Long-term care insurance not qualified
children.text: The term “qualified benefit” shall not include any product which is advertised, marketed, or offered as long-term care insurance.

children.heading: Certain exchange-participating qualified health plans not qualified
children.label: 3 Certain exchange-participating qualified health plans not qualified

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: The term “qualified benefit” shall not include any qualified health plan (as defined in section 1301(a) of the Patient Protection and Affordable Care Act) offered through an Exchange established under section 1311 of such Act.

children.heading: Exception for exchange-eligible employers
children.label: B Exception for exchange-eligible employers
children.text: Subparagraph (A) shall not apply with respect to any employee if such employee’s employer is a qualified employer (as defined in section 1312(f)(2) of the Patient Protection and Affordable Care Act) offering the employee the opportunity to enroll through such an Exchange in a qualified health plan in a group market.

children.heading: Collectively bargained plan not considered discriminatory
children.label: 1 Collectively bargained plan not considered discriminatory
children.text: For purposes of this section, a plan shall not be treated as discriminatory if the plan is maintained under an agreement which the Secretary finds to be a collective bargaining agreement between employee representatives and one or more employers.

children.label: i
children.text: equals 100 percent of the cost of the health benefit coverage under the plan of the majority of the highly compensated participants similarly situated, or

children.label: ii
children.text: equals or exceeds 75 percent of the cost of the health benefit coverage of the participant (similarly situated) having the highest cost health benefit coverage under the plan, and

children.label: B
children.text: contributions or benefits under the plan in excess of those described in subparagraph (A) bear a uniform relationship to compensation.

children.heading: Certain participation eligibility rules not treated as discriminatory
children.label: 3 Certain participation eligibility rules not treated as discriminatory
A
children.text: benefits a group of employees described in section 410(b)(2)(A)(i), and

children.label: i
children.text: No employee is required to complete more than 3 years of employment with the employer or employers maintaining the plan as a condition of participation in the plan, and the employment requirement for each employee is the same.

children.label: ii
children.text: Any employee who has satisfied the employment requirement of clause (i) and who is otherwise entitled to participate in the plan commences participation no later than the first day of the first plan year beginning after the date the employment requirement was satisfied unless the employee was separated from service before the first day of that plan year.

children.heading: Certain controlled groups, etc.
children.label: 4 Certain controlled groups, etc.
children.text: All employees who are treated as employed by a single employer under subsection (b), (c), or (m) of section 414 shall be treated as employed by a single employer for purposes of this section.

children.heading: Special rule for unused benefits in health flexible spending arrangements of individuals called to active duty
children.label: h Special rule for unused benefits in health flexible spending arrangements of individuals called to active duty

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: For purposes of this title, a plan or other arrangement shall not fail to be treated as a cafeteria plan or health flexible spending arrangement (and shall not fail to be treated as an accident or health plan) merely because such arrangement provides for qualified reservist distributions.

children.label: A
children.text: such individual was (by reason of being a member of a reserve component (as defined in , United States Code)) ordered or called to active duty for a period in excess of 179 days or for an indefinite period, andsection 101 of title 37

children.label: B
children.text: such distribution is made during the period beginning on the date of such order or call and ending on the last date that reimbursements could otherwise be made under such arrangement for the plan year which includes the date of such order or call.

children.heading: Limitation on health flexible spending arrangements
children.label: i Limitation on health flexible spending arrangements

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: For purposes of this section, if a benefit is provided under a cafeteria plan through employer contributions to a health flexible spending arrangement, such benefit shall not be treated as a qualified benefit unless the cafeteria plan provides that an employee may not elect for any taxable year to have salary reduction contributions in excess of $2,500 made to such arrangement.

children.label: A
children.text: such amount, multiplied by

children.label: B
children.text: the cost-of-living adjustment determined under section 1(f)(3) for the calendar year in which such taxable year begins by substituting “calendar year 2012” for “calendar year 2016” in subparagraph (A)(ii) thereof.

children.heading: Simple cafeteria plans for small businesses
children.label: j Simple cafeteria plans for small businesses

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: An eligible employer maintaining a simple cafeteria plan with respect to which the requirements of this subsection are met for any year shall be treated as meeting any applicable nondiscrimination requirement during such year.

children.label: A
children.text: which is established and maintained by an eligible employer, and

children.label: B
children.text: with respect to which the contribution requirements of paragraph (3), and the eligibility and participation requirements of paragraph (4), are met.

children.label: i
children.text: a uniform percentage (not less than 2 percent) of the employee’s compensation for the plan year, or

children.label: I
children.text: 6 percent of the employee’s compensation for the plan year, or

children.label: II
children.text: twice the amount of the salary reduction contributions of each qualified employee.

children.heading: Matching contributions on behalf of highly compensated and key employees
children.label: B Matching contributions on behalf of highly compensated and key employees
children.text: The requirements of subparagraph (A)(ii) shall not be treated as met if, under the plan, the rate of contributions with respect to any salary reduction contribution of a highly compensated or key employee at any rate of contribution is greater than that with respect to an employee who is not a highly compensated or key employee.

children.heading: Additional contributions
children.label: C Additional contributions
children.text: Subject to subparagraph (B), nothing in this paragraph shall be treated as prohibiting an employer from making contributions to provide qualified benefits under the plan in addition to contributions required under subparagraph (A).

children.heading: Salary reduction contribution
children.label: i Salary reduction contribution
children.text: The term “salary reduction contribution” means, with respect to a cafeteria plan, any amount which is contributed to the plan at the election of the employee and which is not includible in gross income by reason of this section.

children.heading: Qualified employee
children.label: ii Qualified employee
children.text: The term “qualified employee” means, with respect to a cafeteria plan, any employee who is not a highly compensated or key employee and who is eligible to participate in the plan.

children.heading: Highly compensated employee
children.label: iii Highly compensated employee
children.text: The term “highly compensated employee” has the meaning given such term by section 414(q).

children.heading: Key employee
children.label: iv Key employee
children.text: The term “key employee” has the meaning given such term by section 416(i).

children.label: i
children.text: all employees who had at least 1,000 hours of service for the preceding plan year are eligible to participate, and

children.label: ii
children.text: each employee eligible to participate in the plan may, subject to terms and conditions applicable to all participants, elect any benefit available under the plan.

children.heading: Certain employees may be excluded
children.label: B Certain employees may be excluded
i
children.text: who have not attained the age of 21 before the close of a plan year,

children.label: ii
children.text: who have less than 1 year of service with the employer as of any day during the plan year,

children.label: iii
children.text: who are covered under an agreement which the Secretary of Labor finds to be a collective bargaining agreement if there is evidence that the benefits covered under the cafeteria plan were the subject of good faith bargaining between employee representatives and the employer, or

children.label: iv
children.text: who are described in section 410(b)(3)(C) (relating to nonresident aliens working outside the United States).

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: The term “eligible employer” means, with respect to any year, any employer if such employer employed an average of 100 or fewer employees on business days during either of the 2 preceding years. For purposes of this subparagraph, a year may only be taken into account if the employer was in existence throughout the year.

children.heading: Employers not in existence during preceding year
children.label: B Employers not in existence during preceding year
children.text: If an employer was not in existence throughout the preceding year, the determination under subparagraph (A) shall be based on the average number of employees that it is reasonably expected such employer will employ on business days in the current year.

children.heading: Growing employers retain treatment as small employer
children.label: C Growing employers retain treatment as small employer
I
children.text: an employer was an eligible employer for any year (a “qualified year”), and

children.label: II
children.text: such employer establishes a simple cafeteria plan for its employees for such year,

children.heading: Exception
children.label: ii Exception
children.text: This subparagraph shall cease to apply if the employer employs an average of 200 or more employees on business days during any year preceding any such subsequent year.

children.heading: Predecessors
children.label: i Predecessors
children.text: Any reference in this paragraph to an employer shall include a reference to any predecessor of such employer.

children.heading: Aggregation rules
children.label: ii Aggregation rules
children.text: All persons treated as a single employer under subsection (a) or (b) of section 52, or subsection (n) or () of section 414, shall be treated as one person.o

children.heading: Applicable nondiscrimination requirement
children.label: 6 Applicable nondiscrimination requirement
children.text: For purposes of this subsection, the term “applicable nondiscrimination requirement” means any requirement under subsection (b) of this section, section 79(d), section 105(h), or paragraph (2), (3), (4), or (8) of section 129(d).

children.heading: Compensation
children.label: 7 Compensation
children.text: The term “compensation” has the meaning given such term by section 414(s).

children.heading: Cross reference
children.label: k Cross reference
children.text: For reporting and recordkeeping requirements, see section 6039D.

children.heading: Regulations
children.label: l Regulations
children.text: The Secretary shall prescribe such regulations as may be necessary to carry out the provisions of this section.
